question.label: Voice leverage
question.name: In those leadership meetings, what feels unclear about where your voice has leverage?
question.description: Drills into the specific uncertainty behind her quieter participation in leadership forums.

answer: Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.

assessment.note: She links her quieter participation to unspoken fatigue from constant context switching, suggesting mild lack of clarity about where to focus her voice; also reveals a wellbeing strain worth probing next. [THREAD-DEFERRED] constant context switching/tiredness surfaced but drill cap requires stage advance.

new_queue.name: What's been most on your mind at work lately?

new_queue.name: What have you been saying no to since the reorg?

new_queue.name: What kind of support would actually make this week easier for you?

new_queue.label: Next focus
new_queue.name: Given what we've covered, what do you want to tackle first?

history.answer_excerpt: I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I can feel the shift since the reorg, even though I am trying to keep things stable for the team.
I have been quieter in leadership meetings because I am still figuring out where my voice has most leverage.
Part of me is tired from constant context switching, and I have not really said that out loud.
